Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment And Amend Expert Witness Designation
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. and RASIER-CA, LLC'S Motion To Augment And Amend Expert Witness Designation is granted. The Uber defendants are permitted to augment their expert witness designation to include Dr. Richard Perillo and to delete Dr. Mark Strassberg. Counsel for the Uber defendants' recent learning that Dr. Strassberg is not allowed to receive the testing results of the neuropsychological exams taken by plaintiff Paul Weaver is a sufficient ground for the relief sought. While it is arguable that counsel for the Uber defendants should have learned that Dr. Strassberg was not able to receive the testing results at an earlier time, their failure to do so is not a basis to deprive the Uber defendants of the ability to have an expert regarding the neuropsychological exams taken by Mr. Weaver. Nor has Mr. Weaver shown that he will suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Plaintiff Paul Weaver'S Motion To Strike Defendants Supplemental Experts; Declaration Of Monica Burneikis; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 10/31/17)
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Paul Weaver's Motion To Strike Defendants Supplemental Experts is denied as to Dr. Mark Strassberg and Dr. Winthrop Smith and off calendar as moot as to Dr. Jerome Barakos. Defendants' supplemental designation of Dr. Strassberg and Dr. Smith were in full compliance with the supplemental designation procedure and does not run afoul of Fairfax v. Lords (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 1019, a case where the party seeking to designate supplemental experts had not participated in the initial designation. Nor has Mr. Weaver shown that he will suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. The portion of the motion regarding Dr. Barakos is moot due to defendants' withdrawal of Dr. Barakos as an expert. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
